full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: I want to show that the RSVM2 method fails on nonlinear data because of the linear classifier assumption I identified in Task 1.2. What's a good synthetic dataset for this?
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI confirmed that make_circles (concentric rings) is an ideal choice — the data is perfectly separable by a nonlinear boundary but no linear hyperplane can separate the classes. It also mentioned make_moons as an alternative, but noted make_circles is cleaner because the failure is absolute (0% possible improvement with linear methods) rather than partial.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Confirmed make_circles is perfect — concentric rings that are perfectly separable but not by any hyperplane
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: This directly tests Assumption 3 (linear classifiers) from my Task 1.2 analysis

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me create a decision boundary visualization showing three models side by side — my RSVM2, linear SVM, and RBF SVM — on the circles dataset
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI provided a matplotlib visualization using meshgrid to create a grid of points, predict class labels across the grid with each model, and use contourf to plot the decision regions. It used three subplots side by side with sklearn's SVC predict method.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Got the matplotlib meshgrid visualization template
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Used the meshgrid + contourf approach from the AI but rewrote the prediction function to work with my W matrix format instead of sklearn's API, changed colors and layout
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Standard decision boundary visualization — I adapted it to my specific models
